news.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

news.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

news.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

news.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
news.description: [TECHCRUNCH] Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

news.title: Analysis-AI investment boom puts Big Tech's free cash flow under pressure
news.description: [THE STAR] July 22 (Reuters) - U.S. hyperscalers are starting to ⁠show returns on their artificial intelligence investments, but the rising cost of the buildout is taking a bite out of their free cash flow, ⁠and investors are noticing.

news.title: OpenAI says AI models went rogue during testing, triggering 'unprecedented' breach at startup
news.description: [BANGKOK POST] WASHINGTON — OpenAI said on Tuesday ‌that an autonomous agent powered by its advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models went rogue during a security test and triggered a hack that compromised the infrastructure of AI startup Hugging Face last week.

news.title: Robot AI company Noetra is 'last chance' for Japan, CEO says
news.description: [REUTERS] Noetra, a government-backed company developing a foundational model for physical artificial intelligence and robots, ​is the "last chance" for Japan as it seeks ‌to improve the domestic supply of key technologies, its CEO said in an interview.
